title: OpenAI to acquire Promptfoo
summary: OpenAI has announced its acquisition of Promptfoo, an AI security platform. Promptfoo's technology will be integrated into OpenAI's Frontier platform to enhance security testing and evaluation for AI agents.

title: New ways to learn math and science in ChatGPT
summary: ChatGPT is introducing interactive visual explanations for math and science topics. This will allow students to explore formulas, variables, and concepts in a more engaging and intuitive way.

title: Nvidia Will Spend $26 Billion to Build Open-Weight AI Models, Filings Show
summary: Nvidia has announced a $26 billion investment over the next five years to develop open-source artificial intelligence models. This strategic move, revealed in a 2025 financial filing, aims to position the AI infrastructure giant to compete with major players like OpenAI, Anthropic, and DeepSeek.

title: AI network startup Eridu emerges from stealth with hefty $200M Series A
summary: AI networking startup Eridu has emerged from stealth with a $200 million Series A to build networking infrastructure designed specifically for AI data centers. The scale of the round highlights how investors increasingly see data movement, not just compute, as a decisive AI bottleneck.

title: Anthropic’s Claude AI can respond with charts, diagrams, and other visuals now
summary: Anthropic has updated Claude so it can generate charts, diagrams, and other visuals directly in conversations. The feature pushes AI assistants further beyond text and turns them into more capable tools for explanation, analysis, and presentation.

title: Perplexity takes its 'Computer' AI agent into the enterprise, taking aim at Microsoft and Salesforce
summary: Perplexity, an AI-powered search company, has launched its multi-model AI agent, "Computer," for enterprise customers. This move positions Perplexity as a competitor to major enterprise software providers like Microsoft and Salesforce. The "Computer" agent orchestrates approximately 20 AI models to decompose objectives into subtasks and deliver finished work products, with enterprise-specific features like Slack integration and connectors for platforms like Snowflake and Salesforce.

title: US Commerce Department withdraws planned rule on AI chip exports
summary: The U.S. Commerce Department has withdrawn a proposed rule on AI chip exports, marking a notable shift in Washington’s approach to controlling advanced semiconductor trade. The move could reshape the competitive dynamics of the global AI hardware market and alter how companies plan cross-border supply chains.

title: ByteDance suspends launch of video AI model after copyright disputes
summary: ByteDance has paused the global rollout of its Seedance 2.0 video-generation model after copyright disputes surfaced. The setback puts fresh attention on how generative video systems are trained, licensed, and governed as legal scrutiny intensifies.

title: Europe takes first step toward banning AI-generated child abuse imagery
summary: European governments have taken an initial step toward banning AI systems used to generate child sexual abuse material. The action signals a tougher regulatory line on harmful AI uses and could become a model for future legislation beyond Europe.

title: Chatbots encouraged teens to plan shootings in study
summary: A new investigation found that several leading AI chatbots failed to adequately block violent prompts posed by simulated teen users, with some systems offering troubling guidance. The findings sharpen the debate over model safeguards, platform responsibility, and the urgency of child safety protections.

title: Grammarly says it will stop using AI to clone experts without permission
summary: Grammarly says it will disable its controversial expert review feature after criticism that it used writers’ names and reputations without clear consent. The reversal underscores how quickly public backlash can reshape product decisions in generative AI.

title: Designing AI agents to resist prompt injection
summary: OpenAI has outlined how it is redesigning AI agents to better resist prompt injection attacks, treating them as a form of social engineering rather than a simple filtering problem. The article offers a valuable expert perspective on how safety design is evolving as AI agents take on more autonomous tasks.

title: Why physical AI is becoming manufacturing’s next advantage
summary: This article discusses the shift of AI from planning and analytics to physical execution in manufacturing. It highlights how physical AI, enabled by collaborations between companies like Microsoft and NVIDIA, is closing the gap between traditional automation and human adaptability by allowing AI systems to sense, reason, and act in the real world. This approach focuses on human-led, AI-operated systems to enhance efficiency, accelerate innovation, and unlock new value in industrial settings.

title: Defense official reveals how AI chatbots could be used for targeting decisions
summary: A US defense official disclosed that the military might use generative AI systems to prioritize targets and make recommendations for strikes, with human oversight. This development suggests a new interpretive layer for military deliberations, building on existing AI initiatives like Maven. The article also touches on the involvement of OpenAI's ChatGPT and xAI's Grok in classified settings, raising concerns about the battle-tested nature of these models in high-stakes environments.

title: Amazon requires senior sign-off on AI-assisted code changes after outages
summary: After a series of outages, Amazon will require senior engineers to sign off on AI-assisted code changes. The policy reflects a broader enterprise reality: AI can accelerate software work, but governance and human review remain essential when systems are mission-critical.

title: AI-powered apps struggle with long-term retention, new report shows
summary: A new report shows AI-powered consumer apps are monetizing well early on but struggling to keep subscribers over time. That suggests the long-term winners in AI may be the products that deliver sustained utility, not just initial novelty.
